paper.title_en: The KM3NeT 220 PeV event: a neutrino messenger from the grand unification scale?
paper.abstract_en: The 220~PeV neutrino KM3-230213A, in tension with IceCube and Auger limits unless it is a narrow transient feature, admits a two-body reading: the decay $X \to ν\barν$ of a relic with $m_X = 4.4\times10^{8}$~GeV. The flux fixes $τ/f$, lifetime over dark-matter fraction, and the lifetime fixes an effective coupling $G_{\rm eff} \sim 10^{-46}$~GeV$^{-2}$; for a flavored onium decaying through one electroweak-Higgs insertion the mediator lands near $10^{16}$~GeV, so energy and rate together read out the grand-unified scale. We develop the construction and its tests against the astrophysical alternative.
